reasoning:
For the question to resolve positively, there must be at least one ACLED-eligible fatality in Guyana between February 20, 2026, and March 21, 2026 (the 30 days before March 22). As of March 15, 2026, roughly 23 days of this window have already passed. Extensive searches across local Guyanese news (Stabroek News, Kaieteur News, Demerara Waves) show zero reports of political violence, riots, clashes with armed groups, or border violence with Venezuela during this time frame. While standard homicides have occurred (e.g., an altercation on Feb 22), ACLED explicitly excludes interpersonal and non-organized criminal violence for Guyana. 

The baseline average of 0.583 fatalities/month (~7 per year) was highly skewed by an isolated cluster of riot fatalities in April/May 2025 following the death of an 11-year-old girl. Without those isolated riots, the background rate of ACLED fatalities in Guyana is extremely low. There is a small heightened baseline risk due to regional instability (a major US intervention in Venezuela occurred in January 2026), but no spillover clashes have occurred in Guyana in the past two months. Therefore, with only 7 days remaining in the resolution period, the chance of a qualifying fatal event occurring is estimated to be roughly 5-6%. Combining this with a very small (~1%) chance that a qualifying event already occurred but eluded news searches, a final probability estimate of 6% is well placed.
